questions.question: Define the F-distribution. State its probability density function and list its important properties including mean, variance, and mode.
keyPoints: Definition as ratio of two independent chi-square variables divided by their degrees of freedom
PDF formula involving the Beta function
Parameters d1 (numerator df) and d2 (denominator df)
Mean = d2/(d2-2) for d2 > 2
Variance formula for d2 > 4
Mode formula for d1 > 2
Non-negative, right-skewed, reciprocal property

questions.question: Two random samples of sizes 10 and 12 drawn from two normal populations have sample variances 30 and 12 respectively. Test at the 5% significance level whether the two population variances are equal.
keyPoints: State null and alternative hypotheses (H0: sigma1^2 = sigma2^2)
Compute F = larger variance / smaller variance = 30/12 = 2.5
Degrees of freedom: d1 = 9, d2 = 11
Look up critical value F_0.025(9,11) from table
Compare computed F with critical value and draw conclusion

questions.question: Explain the relationship between the F-distribution, t-distribution, and chi-square distribution with relevant formulae.
keyPoints: F is ratio of two independent chi-square variables divided by their df
If T ~ t(n), then T^2 ~ F(1, n)
Reciprocal property: 1/F(d1,d2) ~ F(d2,d1)
For large df, F approximates normal distribution
Chi-square is a building block for the F-distribution

questions.question: In an ANOVA test with 4 groups and a total of 40 observations, the mean square between groups (MSB) is 54 and the mean square within groups (MSW) is 18. Perform the F-test at the 5% level of significance and state your conclusion.
keyPoints: Compute F = MSB/MSW = 54/18 = 3.0
Degrees of freedom: d1 = k-1 = 3, d2 = N-k = 36
Look up critical value F_0.05(3, 36) from the table
Compare F-statistic with critical value
Conclude whether group means are significantly different

questions.question: Derive the mean of the F-distribution with d1 and d2 degrees of freedom. Also compute the mean and variance of F(6, 10).
keyPoints: Start from definition F = (X1/d1)/(X2/d2) and use expectation properties
Show E(F) = d2/(d2-2) for d2 > 2
